name: color-token-manager
description: Extract hardcoded colors into tokens, manage colors.ts, preview refactors, WCAG contrast checks, and export design tokens for React Native and TypeScript.

colorTokenManager.colorsFilePath.description: Custom path to colors.ts, relative to the workspace folder of the active file (multi-root safe). Example: src/theme/colors.ts

colorTokenManager.importPath.description: Custom import path for colors. If empty, calculate relative import path automatically.

colorTokenManager.generatedNamePrefix.description: Optional prefix for generated token names.

colorTokenManager.namingStrategy.description: Controls generated token names. semantic can generate nested paths like button.background; contextValue includes context and color value; valueOnly uses only the color value.

colorTokenManager.autoReplaceExistingCol.description: When true, replace hardcoded colors with existing matching tokens automatically.

colorTokenManager.excludeGlobs.description: Additional glob patterns to skip during folder preview and extraction.

colorTokenManager.createSemanticAliases.description: When true, create semantic alias tokens like buttonBackground: colors.primary for existing color values used in a new context.

colorTokenManager.createBackupsBeforeFol.description: When true, write timestamped backups before folder-wide extraction applies changes.

colorTokenManager.enableInlineSwatches.description: When true, show inline color swatches next to hardcoded colors in supported source files.

colorTokenManager.enableContrastDiagnost.description: When true, warn in colors.ts when text-like tokens do not meet the configured WCAG contrast target against background tokens.

colorTokenManager.contrastLevel.description: WCAG contrast level used by color token diagnostics. AA requires 4.5:1 and AAA requires 7:1 for normal text.

colorTokenManager.contrastBackgroundToke.description: Optional token keys to use as contrast backgrounds, such as background.white. If empty, background-like tokens are detected automatically.

colorTokenManager.tokenLayerMode.description: When semanticFirst, new extracted colors create primitive tokens and semantic aliases, such as primitive.gray.gray500 and semantic.text.muted.

colorTokenManager.themeTokenPrefix.description: Optional theme prefix for generated semantic token paths. Example: dark creates colors.dark.background.primary in flat mode or colors.semantic.dark.background.primary in semantic-first mode.

colorTokenManager.importStyle.description: Deprecated. Use colorTokenManager.importMode instead. Removed in v1.0.0.
colorTokenManager.importStyle.deprecationMessage: Use colorTokenManager.importMode instead. This setting will be removed in v1.0.0.

colorTokenManager.importMode.description: Controls generated import syntax for colors.

colorTokenManager.importIdentifier.description: Identifier to use for inserted color references and imports.

colorTokenManager.extractEmbeddedColors.description: When true, also extract colors embedded inside longer quoted CSS-like strings such as boxShadow: '0 0 4px #000'.

scripts.compile: npm run check && npm run bundle
scripts.test: npm run test:compile && npm run test:run
scripts.test:run: node --require ./test/setup.cjs --test out-test/test/smoke.test.js out-test/test/colorFile.test.js out-test/test/colorExtractor.test.js out-test/test/globUtils.test.js out-test/test/workspaceUtils.test.js
scripts.test:integration: npm run compile && npm run test:integration:compile && node ./out-test/test/runTest.js
